title: Push to a Remote Repository
description: Write the shell commands to add a remote, verify it, push with upstream tracking, fetch, and pull.
starterCode:
# Write shell commands to:
# 1. Add a remote named origin pointing to
#    https://github.com/alice/my-project.git
# 2. Verify the remotes are set up correctly (list with URLs)
# 3. Push the main branch to origin and set upstream tracking (-u)
# 4. Fetch updates from origin
# 5. Pull the latest changes
solutionCode:
git remote add origin https://github.com/alice/my-project.git
git remote -v
git push -u origin main
git fetch origin
git pull
hints: git remote add <name> <url> registers a new remote
git remote -v lists all remotes with their fetch and push URLs
git push -u origin main pushes and sets the upstream tracking branch
git fetch origin downloads new data without modifying your local branches
git pull integrates the fetched remote changes into your current branch

validationRules.errorMessage: Add the remote with: git remote add origin <url>
Verify your remotes with git remote -v
Push with -u to set upstream tracking: git push -u origin main
Download remote updates with git fetch
Integrate remote changes with git pull

simulatedOutput.success:

remote: https://github.com/alice/my-project.git (fetch)
remote: https://github.com/alice/my-project.git (push)

Branch 'main' set up to track remote branch 'main' from 'origin'.
To https://github.com/alice/my-project.git
 * [new branch]      main -> main

Fetching origin
Already up to date.
simulatedOutput.failure: Some required steps are missing. Make sure you run git remote add origin, git remote -v, git push -u origin, git fetch, and git pull.
